toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of messages (last 80 lines)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Last 80 lines (approximately last 3-5 messages)
    tail -n 80 "$transcript_path" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output, mode
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"
    local mode="$3"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # For topic-only mode, select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    if [ "$mode" = "topic-only" ]; then
        local clarity
        clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

        local snarky_comment
        if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
            snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
        else
            snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
        fi

        # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
        json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')
    fi

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    # For full-analytics mode, write analytics.json
    if [ "$mode" = "full-analytics" ]; then
        local analytics_file="${session_dir}/analytics.json"
        echo "$json_output" > "$analytics_file"
        log_info "Analytics file updated: $analytics_file"
    fi

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local mode=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODE")
    mode=${mode:-topic-only}

    local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
    model=${model:-haiku-4.5}

    log_debug "Analysis mode: $mode, model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$mode" "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$mode" "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Invoke Claude
    log_debug "Invoking Claude for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json (and analytics.json if full mode)
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output" "$mode"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Launch sleeper in background directly
    local log_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.log"

    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
    local source_root
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    nohup bash -c "
        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'

        # Source common and feature libraries from real location
        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'
        source '$source_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging
        log_init '$session_id'

        # Run sleeper loop
        topic_extraction_sleeper_loop '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Sleeper polling loop (runs as background process)
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
topic_extraction_sleeper_loop() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Sleeper loop started for session: $session_id"

    # Get configuration
    local max_duration=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION")
    max_duration=${max_duration:-600}

    local min_size_change=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE")
    min_size_change=${min_size_change:-500}

    local min_interval=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL")
    min_interval=${min_interval:-10}

    local min_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP")
    min_sleep=${min_sleep:-2}

    local max_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP")
    max_sleep=${max_sleep:-20}

    # Initialize state
    local last_activity_time=$(date +%s)
    local last_size=0
    local last_analysis_time=0

    # Main polling loop
    while true; do
        # Check inactivity timeout
        local current_time=$(date +%s)
        local inactive_duration=$((current_time - last_activity_time))

        if [ $inactive_duration -ge $max_duration ]; then
            log_info "Sleeper inactivity timeout reached ($max_duration seconds), exiting"
            break
        fi

        # Get current transcript size
        local current_size
        current_size=$(util_get_file_size "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

        # Check if transcript changed significantly
        local size_delta=$((current_size - last_size))
        local time_since_analysis=$((current_time - last_analysis_time))

        if [ $size_delta -ge $min_size_change ] && [ $time_since_analysis -ge $min_interval ]; then
            log_debug "Transcript changed by $size_delta bytes, triggering analysis"

            # Update activity timestamp
            last_activity_time=$current_time

            # Run analysis
            if topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
                last_analysis_time=$current_time
            fi

            last_size=$current_size
        fi

        # Determine sleep interval based on clarity score
        # Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min_sleep and max_sleep)
        local clarity
        clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

        local sleep_interval=$min_sleep
        if [ -n "$clarity" ] && [ "$clarity" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
            # Calculate: clarity * 2
            sleep_interval=$((clarity * 2))

            # Cap at max_sleep
            if [ $sleep_interval -gt $max_sleep ]; then
                sleep_interval=$max_sleep
            fi

            # Ensure minimum
            if [ $sleep_interval -lt $min_sleep ]; then
                sleep_interval=$min_sleep
            fi

            log_debug "Dynamic sleep interval: ${sleep_interval}s (clarity=${clarity})"
        else
            log_debug "No clarity score, using minimum sleep: ${min_sleep}s"
        fi

        # Update last size
        last_size=$current_size

        # Sleep
        sleep $sleep_interval
    done

    log_info "Sleeper loop exited for session: $session_id"

    # Clean up PID file
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"
    rm -f "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null || true

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get session directory
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
    local source_root
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Launch resume generation in background directly
    nohup bash -c "
        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'

        # Source common library from real location
        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging
        log_init '$session_id'

        log_info 'Starting resume generation for session: $session_id'

        # Read current topic.json
        topic_file='$session_dir/topic.json'
        if [ ! -f \"\$topic_file\" ]; then
            log_error 'Topic file not found for resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        current_topic=\$(cat \"\$topic_file\")

        # Extract transcript excerpt (last ~3-5 messages, similar to topic-only)
        if [ -f '$transcript_path' ]; then
            transcript_excerpt=\$(tail -n 80 '$transcript_path' | jq -s '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')
        else
            log_warn 'Transcript not found, using empty excerpt'
            transcript_excerpt='[]'
        fi

        # Load prompt template
        prompt_file='$sidekick_root/features/prompts/generate-resume.txt'
        if [ ! -f \"\$prompt_file\" ]; then
            log_error 'Resume prompt template not found: \$prompt_file'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Build prompt with both topic and transcript
        prompt=\$(cat \"\$prompt_file\")
        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{CURRENT_TOPIC\\}/\$current_topic}\"
        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{TRANSCRIPT\\}/\$transcript_excerpt}\"

        # Get resume model from config
        resume_model=\$(config_get 'RESUME_MODEL')
        resume_model=\${resume_model:-haiku}

        log_debug 'Invoking Claude for resume generation (model: \$resume_model)'

        # Invoke Claude
        if ! llm_output=\$(claude_invoke \"\$resume_model\" \"\$prompt\" 30); then
            log_error 'Claude invocation failed for resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Extract JSON from output
        if ! resume_json=\$(claude_extract_json \"\$llm_output\"); then
            log_error 'Failed to extract JSON from resume generation output'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Validate JSON
        if ! echo \"\$resume_json\" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
            log_error 'Invalid JSON from resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Write resume.json
        resume_file='$session_dir/resume.json'
        echo \"\$resume_json\" > \"\$resume_file\"

        log_info 'Resume file created: \$resume_file'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

        return 1

    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json (and analytics.json if full mode)
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output" "$mode"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of messages (last 80 lines)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Last 80 lines (approximately last 3-5 messages)
    tail -n 80 "$transcript_path" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local mode=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODE")
    mode=${mode:-topic-only}

    local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
    model=${model:-haiku-4.5}

    log_debug "Analysis mode: $mode, model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$mode" "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$mode" "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Invoke Claude
    log_debug "Invoking Claude for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json (and analytics.json if full mode)
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output" "$mode"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Launch sleeper in background directly
    local log_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.log"

    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
    local source_root
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    nohup bash -c "
        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'

        # Source common and feature libraries from real location
        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'
        source '$source_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging
        log_init '$session_id'

        # Run sleeper loop
        topic_extraction_sleeper_loop '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Sleeper polling loop (runs as background process)
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
topic_extraction_sleeper_loop() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Sleeper loop started for session: $session_id"

    # Get configuration
    local max_duration=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION")
    max_duration=${max_duration:-600}

    local min_size_change=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE")
    min_size_change=${min_size_change:-500}

    local min_interval=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL")
    min_interval=${min_interval:-10}

    local min_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP")
    min_sleep=${min_sleep:-2}

    local max_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP")
    max_sleep=${max_sleep:-20}

    # Initialize state
    local last_activity_time=$(date +%s)
    local last_size=0
    local last_analysis_time=0

    # Main polling loop
    while true; do
        # Check inactivity timeout
        local current_time=$(date +%s)
        local inactive_duration=$((current_time - last_activity_time))

        if [ $inactive_duration -ge $max_duration ]; then
            log_info "Sleeper inactivity timeout reached ($max_duration seconds), exiting"
            break
        fi

        # Get current transcript size
        local current_size
        current_size=$(util_get_file_size "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

        # Check if transcript changed significantly
        local size_delta=$((current_size - last_size))
        local time_since_analysis=$((current_time - last_analysis_time))

        if [ $size_delta -ge $min_size_change ] && [ $time_since_analysis -ge $min_interval ]; then
            log_debug "Transcript changed by $size_delta bytes, triggering analysis"

            # Update activity timestamp
            last_activity_time=$current_time

            # Run analysis
            if topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
                last_analysis_time=$current_time
            fi

            last_size=$current_size
        fi

        # Determine sleep interval based on clarity score
        # Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min_sleep and max_sleep)
        local clarity
        clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

        local sleep_interval=$min_sleep
        if [ -n "$clarity" ] && [ "$clarity" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
            # Calculate: clarity * 2
            sleep_interval=$((clarity * 2))

            # Cap at max_sleep
            if [ $sleep_interval -gt $max_sleep ]; then
                sleep_interval=$max_sleep
            fi

            # Ensure minimum
            if [ $sleep_interval -lt $min_sleep ]; then
                sleep_interval=$min_sleep
            fi

            log_debug "Dynamic sleep interval: ${sleep_interval}s (clarity=${clarity})"
        else
            log_debug "No clarity score, using minimum sleep: ${min_sleep}s"
        fi

        # Update last size
        last_size=$current_size

        # Sleep
        sleep $sleep_interval
    done

    log_info "Sleeper loop exited for session: $session_id"

    # Clean up PID file
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"
    rm -f "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null || true

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get session directory
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
    local source_root
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Launch resume generation in background directly
    nohup bash -c "
        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'

        # Source common library from real location
        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging
        log_init '$session_id'

        log_info 'Starting resume generation for session: $session_id'

        # Read current topic.json
        topic_file='$session_dir/topic.json'
        if [ ! -f \"\$topic_file\" ]; then
            log_error 'Topic file not found for resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        current_topic=\$(cat \"\$topic_file\")

        # Extract transcript excerpt (last ~3-5 messages, similar to topic-only)
        if [ -f '$transcript_path' ]; then
            transcript_excerpt=\$(tail -n 80 '$transcript_path' | jq -s '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')
        else
            log_warn 'Transcript not found, using empty excerpt'
            transcript_excerpt='[]'
        fi

        # Load prompt template
        prompt_file='$sidekick_root/features/prompts/generate-resume.txt'
        if [ ! -f \"\$prompt_file\" ]; then
            log_error 'Resume prompt template not found: \$prompt_file'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Build prompt with both topic and transcript
        prompt=\$(cat \"\$prompt_file\")
        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{CURRENT_TOPIC\\}/\$current_topic}\"
        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{TRANSCRIPT\\}/\$transcript_excerpt}\"

        # Get resume model from config
        resume_model=\$(config_get 'RESUME_MODEL')
        resume_model=\${resume_model:-haiku}

        log_debug 'Invoking Claude for resume generation (model: \$resume_model)'

        # Invoke Claude
        if ! llm_output=\$(claude_invoke \"\$resume_model\" \"\$prompt\" 30); then
            log_error 'Claude invocation failed for resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Extract JSON from output
        if ! resume_json=\$(claude_extract_json \"\$llm_output\"); then
            log_error 'Failed to extract JSON from resume generation output'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Validate JSON
        if ! echo \"\$resume_json\" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
            log_error 'Invalid JSON from resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Write resume.json
        resume_file='$session_dir/resume.json'
        echo \"\$resume_json\" > \"\$resume_file\"

        log_info 'Resume file created: \$resume_file'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting and static reminders
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Analysis mode: topic-only | incremental | full-analytics
#   topic-only: Fast topic detection (~2s, minimal tokens)
#   incremental: Recent context analysis (~4s)
#   full-analytics: Comprehensive insights (~10s)
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
RESUME_MODEL=haiku

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# Enhanced statusline rendering with tokens, cost, duration, git branch, and topic
#
# Functions:
#   feature_statusline_render() - Main entry point for statusline generation
#   _statusline_format_cost()   - Format cost with colors
#   _statusline_format_duration() - Format duration with colors
#   _statusline_format_tokens()  - Format tokens with colors and emoji
#   _statusline_get_topic()      - Extract topic from analytics
#   _statusline_get_git_branch() - Get formatted git branch
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Format cost in human-readable way with colors
# Args: cost_usd (float)
# Output: Formatted cost string
_statusline_format_cost() {
    local cost="$1"

    if [[ "$cost" == "null" ]] || [[ -z "$cost" ]] || [[ "$cost" == "0" ]] || [[ "$cost" == "0.0" ]]; then
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}--${COLOR_RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Convert to cents for easier handling
    local cents=$(printf "%.0f" "$(echo "$cost * 100" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo "0")")

    if [[ $cents -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}<1¢${COLOR_RESET}"
    elif [[ $cents -lt 100 ]]; then
        printf "${COLOR_YELLOW}%d¢${COLOR_RESET}" "$cents"
    elif [[ $cents -lt 10000 ]]; then  # Less than $100
        printf "${COLOR_YELLOW}\$%.2f${COLOR_RESET}" "$(echo "scale=2; $cents/100" | bc -l)"
    else
        printf "${COLOR_RED}\$%.2f${COLOR_RESET}" "$(echo "scale=2; $cents/100" | bc -l)"
    fi
}

# Format duration in human-readable way with colors
# Args: duration_ms (integer)
# Output: Formatted duration string
_statusline_format_duration() {
    local ms="$1"

    if [[ "$ms" == "null" ]] || [[ -z "$ms" ]] || [[ "$ms" == "0" ]]; then
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}--${COLOR_RESET}"
        return
    fi

    local seconds=$(echo "scale=1; $ms/1000" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Handle different time ranges with appropriate colors and units
    if (( $(echo "$seconds < 1" | bc -l) )); then
        printf "${COLOR_GREEN}%dms${COLOR_RESET}" "$ms"
    elif (( $(echo "$seconds < 60" | bc -l) )); then
        if (( $(echo "$seconds < 10" | bc -l) )); then
            printf "${COLOR_GREEN}%.1fs${COLOR_RESET}" "$seconds"
        else
            printf "${COLOR_YELLOW}%.0fs${COLOR_RESET}" "$seconds"
        fi
    elif (( $(echo "$seconds < 3600" | bc -l) )); then  # Less than 1 hour
        local minutes=$(echo "scale=1; $seconds/60" | bc -l)
        printf "${COLOR_YELLOW}%.1fm${COLOR_RESET}" "$minutes"
    else  # 1 hour or more
        local hours=$(echo "scale=1; $seconds/3600" | bc -l)
        printf "${COLOR_RED}%.1fh${COLOR_RESET}" "$hours"
    fi
}

# Format token count with K notation, color, and emoji
# Args: total_tokens (integer), percentage (integer)
# Output: Formatted tokens string
_statusline_format_tokens() {
    local total_tokens="$1"
    local percentage="$2"

    if [ "$total_tokens" -eq 0 ]; then
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}0${COLOR_RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Format with K notation
    local token_display
    if [ $total_tokens -ge 1000 ]; then
        token_display=$(echo "scale=1; $total_tokens / 1000" | bc)"K"
    else
        token_display="$total_tokens"
    fi

    # Color based on percentage
    if [ $percentage -ge 90 ]; then
        printf "${COLOR_RED}🪙 %s${COLOR_RESET}" "$token_display"
    elif [ $percentage -ge 70 ]; then
        printf "${COLOR_YELLOW}🪙 %s${COLOR_RESET}" "$token_display"
    else
        printf "${COLOR_GREEN}🪙 %s${COLOR_RESET}" "$token_display"
    fi
}

# Extract session topic from topic.json with formatting
# Args: session_id, project_dir
# Output: Formatted topic string
_statusline_get_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ "$session_id" == "null" ]; then
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}--${COLOR_RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Get session directory from project-level .sidekick
    local topic_file="${project_dir}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/topic.json"

    # Check for JSON topic file
    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}--${COLOR_RESET}"
        return
    fi

    # Parse JSON fields
    local task_ids=$(jq -r '.task_ids // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null)
    local initial_goal=$(jq -r '.initial_goal // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null)
    local current_objective=$(jq -r '.current_objective // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null)
    local clarity_score=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 5' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null)

    # Choose snarky comment based on clarity score (7+ = high clarity)
    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity_score" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(jq -r '.snarky_comment // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null)
    else
        snarky_comment=""
    fi

    # Build output: [$tasks]: $initial_goal ($current_objective)\n$snarky_comment
    local output=""

    # Add task IDs prefix if present (skip if empty array)
    if [ -n "$task_ids" ] && [ "$task_ids" != "[]" ]; then
        output="${COLOR_CYAN}[${task_ids}]${COLOR_RESET}: "
    fi

    # Add goals with separator
    if [ -n "$initial_goal" ]; then
        output="${output}${COLOR_MAGENTA}${initial_goal}${COLOR_RESET}"
    fi
    if [ -n "$current_objective" ]; then
        if [ -n "$initial_goal" ]; then
            output="${output} ${COLOR_DIM}${COLOR_RESET}${COLOR_MAGENTA}(${current_objective})${COLOR_RESET}"
        else
            output="${output}${COLOR_MAGENTA}${current_objective}${COLOR_RESET}"
        fi
    fi

    # Add snarky comment on new line
    if [ -n "$snarky_comment" ]; then
        output="${output}\n${COLOR_YELLOW}${snarky_comment}${COLOR_RESET}"
    fi

    # Output or fallback to default
    if [ -n "$output" ]; then
        printf "$output"
    else
        printf "${COLOR_DIM}--${COLOR_RESET}"
    fi
}

# Get formatted git branch with colors
# Args: project_dir
# Output: Formatted git branch string (with leading separator)
_statusline_get_git_branch() {
    local project_dir="$1"

    # Change to project directory if specified
    if [ -n "$project_dir" ] && [ -d "$project_dir" ]; then
        cd "$project_dir"
    fi

    # Check if in git repo
    if ! git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo ""
        return
    fi

    local branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$branch" ]; then
        echo ""
        return
    fi

    # Color branch based on name patterns
    local git_branch
    if [[ "$branch" == "main" ]] || [[ "$branch" == "master" ]]; then
        git_branch=" ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_GREEN}⎇ ${branch}${COLOR_RESET}"
    elif [[ "$branch" == feature/* ]] || [[ "$branch" == feat/* ]]; then
        git_branch=" ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_BLUE}⎇ ${branch}${COLOR_RESET}"
    elif [[ "$branch" == hotfix/* ]] || [[ "$branch" == fix/* ]]; then
        git_branch=" ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_RED}⎇ ${branch}${COLOR_RESET}"
    else
        git_branch=" ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_MAGENTA}⎇ ${branch}${COLOR_RESET}"
    fi

    echo "$git_branch"
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Render enhanced statusline with all information
# Args: input_json - JSON string with statusline data
#
# Input JSON:
#   {
#     "session_id": "...",
#     "model": {"display_name": "..."},
#     "workspace": {"current_dir": "...", "project_dir": "..."},
#     "cost": {"total_cost_usd": 1.23},
#     "duration_ms": 12500,
#     "transcript_path": "..."
#   }
#
# Output: Formatted statusline (single line or multi-line with topic)
feature_statusline_render() {
    local input="$1"

    log_debug "Rendering statusline"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "statusline"; then
        log_debug "Statusline feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON input for statusline"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract values from JSON
    local session_id=$(json_get "$input" ".session_id")
    local model=$(json_get "$input" ".model.display_name")
    local current_dir=$(json_get "$input" ".workspace.current_dir")
    local project_dir=$(json_get "$input" ".workspace.project_dir")
    local cost=$(json_get "$input" ".cost.total_cost_usd // \"0\"")
    local duration_ms=$(json_get "$input" ".duration_ms // \"0\"")
    local transcript_path=$(json_get "$input" ".transcript_path")

    log_debug "Statusline input: session_id=$session_id, model=$model, dir=$current_dir"

    # Calculate tokens from transcript
    local total_tokens=0
    if [ -n "$transcript_path" ] && [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        total_tokens=$(util_calculate_tokens "$transcript_path")
        log_debug "Calculated tokens: $total_tokens"
    else
        log_debug "No transcript path or file not found: $transcript_path"
    fi

    # Get token threshold from config
    local threshold=$(config_get "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD")
    threshold=${threshold:-160000}

    # Calculate percentage
    local percentage=$((total_tokens * 100 / threshold))
    if [ $percentage -gt 100 ]; then
        percentage=100
    fi

    # Format the values
    local tokens_formatted=$(_statusline_format_tokens "$total_tokens" "$percentage")
    local topic_formatted=$(_statusline_get_topic "$session_id" "$project_dir")
    local git_branch=$(_statusline_get_git_branch "$project_dir")

    # Get directory name
    local dir_name="${current_dir##*/}"

    # Build the final statusline
    # Format: [Model] | 🪙 tokens | percentage | 📁 dir | ⎇ branch | topic
    printf "${COLOR_BOLD}${COLOR_BLUE}[${model}]${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${tokens_formatted} ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_CYAN}%d%%${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${COLOR_CYAN}📁 ${dir_name}${COLOR_RESET}${git_branch} ${COLOR_DIM}|${COLOR_RESET} ${topic_formatted}\n" "$percentage"

    log_debug "Statusline rendered successfully"
    return 0
}

#!/bin/bash
# test-topic-extraction.sh - Unit tests for topic extraction feature
#
# Tests the TOPIC EXTRACTION feature from features/topic-extraction.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR"

    # Source common.sh first
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true

    # Override _SIDEKICK_ROOT AFTER sourcing (common.sh sets it during source)
    export _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$TEST_DIR"

    # Copy prompts to test directory
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/features/prompts"
    cp "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/features/prompts/"*.txt "$TEST_DIR/features/prompts/" 2>/dev/null || true

    # Create config.defaults for background processes
    cat >> "$TEST_DIR/config.defaults" <<'EOFCONFIG'
# Test configuration
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku-4.5
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20
RESUME_MODEL=haiku
LOG_LEVEL=error
EOFCONFIG

    # Set up config for current process
    export FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
    export TOPIC_MODE=topic-only
    export TOPIC_MODEL=haiku-4.5
    export TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
    export TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1
    export TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
    export SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
    export SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
    export SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
    export SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20
    export LOG_LEVEL=error  # Suppress logs during tests

    # Create mock Claude CLI
    MOCK_CLAUDE="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOFCLAUDE'
#!/bin/bash
# Mock Claude CLI that returns markdown-wrapped JSON (like real Claude)
cat <<'EOF'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-session",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "task_ids": ["TEST-001"],
  "initial_goal": "Test goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing",
  "clarity_score": 8,
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "snarky_comment": "Mock snark"
}
```
EOF
EOFCLAUDE
    chmod +x "$MOCK_CLAUDE"
    export CLAUDE_BIN="$MOCK_CLAUDE"

    # Source topic-extraction.sh (will be implemented)
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    if [ -f "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/features/topic-extraction.sh" ]; then
        source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/features/topic-extraction.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    # Kill ALL sleeper processes started during tests
    find "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions" -name "sleeper.pid" 2>/dev/null | while read pidfile; do
        if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
            local pid=$(cat "$pidfile" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
            if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
                kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
                pkill -9 -P "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
            fi
        fi
    done
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# TESTS: topic_extraction_get_clarity()
# ============================================================================

test_get_clarity_from_valid_file() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_get_clarity &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    local session_id="test-session-clarity"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/topic.json" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "clarity_score": 9
}
EOF

    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")
    [ "$clarity" = "9" ]
}

test_get_clarity_missing_file() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_get_clarity &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "nonexistent-session")
    # Should return default or empty
    [ -z "$clarity" ] || [ "$clarity" = "5" ]
}

test_get_clarity_invalid_json() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_get_clarity &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    local session_id="test-session-invalid"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    echo "invalid json" > "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/topic.json"

    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || echo "5")
    # Should return default on error
    [ "$clarity" = "5" ]
}

# ============================================================================
# TESTS: topic_extraction_analyze()
# ============================================================================

test_analyze_creates_topic_file() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_analyze &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    local session_id="test-session-analyze"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    # Create mock transcript
    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript.jsonl"
    cat > "$transcript" <<EOF
{"role":"user","content":"Let's implement feature X"}
{"role":"assistant","content":"Sure, I'll help with that"}
EOF

    topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"

    # Should have created topic.json
    [ -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/topic.json" ]

    # Should contain expected fields
    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score' "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/topic.json")
    [ -n "$clarity" ]
}

test_analyze_respects_mode_topic_only() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_analyze &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    export TOPIC_MODE=topic-only

    local session_id="test-session-topic-only"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    # Create mock transcript
    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"test"}' > "$transcript"

    topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"

    # Should have topic.json but not analytics.json
    [ -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/topic.json" ]
    [ ! -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/analytics.json" ]
}

test_analyze_respects_mode_full_analytics() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_analyze &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    export TOPIC_MODE=full-analytics

    local session_id="test-session-full"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    # Create mock transcript
    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"test"}' > "$transcript"

    # Update mock Claude to return complete analytics JSON (markdown-wrapped)
    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOFCLAUDE'
#!/bin/bash
cat <<'EOF'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-session",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "task_ids": ["TEST-001"],
  "initial_goal": "Test goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing",
```

  "clarity_score": 9
}
EOF

    # Create transcript

    echo '{"role":"user","content":"test"}' > "$transcript"

    # Test count 5 (should not analyze)

        # Function returned true, meaning analysis is due

    fi

test_sleeper_start_creates_pid_file() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_sleeper_start &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    local session_id="test-session-sleeper"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"test"}' > "$transcript"

    topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"

    # Should have created PID file
    sleep 0.5  # Give it time to start
    [ -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" ]

    # Should have a valid PID
    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid")
    [ -n "$pid" ]

    # Process should be running
    ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true  # Don't fail if already exited

    # Clean up
    pkill -P "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
}

test_sleeper_start_doesnt_duplicate() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_sleeper_start &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    local session_id="test-session-sleeper-dup"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"test"}' > "$transcript"

    # Start first sleeper
    topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"
    sleep 0.5

    local pid1
    pid1=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    # Try to start second sleeper
    topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"
    sleep 0.5

    local pid2
    pid2=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    # PIDs should be the same (didn't start duplicate)
    [ "$pid1" = "$pid2" ]

    # Clean up
    [ -n "$pid1" ] && pkill -P "$pid1" 2>/dev/null || true
}

test_sleeper_disabled() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_sleeper_start &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Save original value and restore after test
    local orig_sleeper_enabled="${SLEEPER_ENABLED:-true}"
    export SLEEPER_ENABLED=false

    local session_id="test-session-sleeper-disabled"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    sleep 0.5

    # Should NOT have created PID file
    local result=0
    [ ! -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" ] || result=1

    # Restore original value
    export SLEEPER_ENABLED="$orig_sleeper_enabled"

    return $result
}

# TESTS: Sleeper Inactivity Timeout Behavior
# ============================================================================

test_sleeper_exits_after_inactivity_timeout() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet
    if ! command -v topic_extraction_sleeper_start &> /dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Explicitly enable sleeper (may have been disabled by previous test)
    export SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

    # Clean up any leftover sleepers from previous tests
    pkill -9 -f "topic_extraction_sleeper_loop" 2>/dev/null || true
    sleep 0.2

    # Write config overrides in project sidekick.conf for background processes
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=5
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=1
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=2
EOF

    # Set short timeout for testing (for current process)
    export SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=5
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=1
    export SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=2

    local session_id="test-session-inactivity-exit"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    # Create static transcript (no changes during test)
    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript-static.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"test"}' > "$transcript"

    # Start sleeper
    topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"
    sleep 1  # Give sleeper time to fully start

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    [ -n "$pid" ] || {
        echo "ERROR: PID file empty or missing" >&2
        return 1
    }

    # Verify sleeper is initially running
    ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1 || {
        echo "ERROR: Sleeper not running initially (PID=$pid)" >&2
        echo "Sleeper log:" >&2
        cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.log" 2>&1 | tail -20 >&2 || echo "No log available" >&2
        echo "Session log:" >&2
        cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sidekick.log" 2>&1 | tail -20 >&2 || echo "No session log" >&2
        return 1
    }

    # Wait beyond timeout (6 seconds > 5 second timeout)
    sleep 6

    # Verify sleeper has exited
    if ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: Sleeper still running after 6s (PID=$pid, expected timeout=5s)" >&2
        echo "Sleeper log:" >&2
        cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.log" 2>&1 | tail -20 >&2 || true
        return 1
    fi

    # Verify log shows inactivity timeout
    local log_file="$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.log"
    if [ -f "$log_file" ]; then
        grep -q "inactivity timeout" "$log_file" || true
    fi

    # PID file should be cleaned up
    [ ! -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" ]
}

test_sleeper_stays_alive_with_ongoing_activity() {
    # Skip if function doesn't exist yet

    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'

SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=1
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=2
EOF

    # Set short timeout for testing (for current process)
    export SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=5
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=1
    export SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=2

    local session_id="test-session-ongoing-activity"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    # Create initial transcript
    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript-active.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"initial content"}' > "$transcript"

    # Start sleeper
    topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"
    sleep 0.5

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    [ -n "$pid" ] || return 1

    # Add significant activity every 3 seconds for 12 seconds (2.4x max_duration)
    for i in {1..4}; do
        sleep 3

        # Append >500 bytes of data to trigger activity detection
        local padding=$(printf '%*s' 600 '' | tr ' ' 'X')
        echo "{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"update $i $padding\"}" >> "$transcript"
    done

    # After 12 seconds of regular activity, sleeper should still be running
    ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1

    # Clean up
    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid"
}

    sleep 0.5

    [ -n "$pid" ] || return 1

    # Wait 4 seconds (almost timed out at 5 second limit)
    sleep 4

    # Add significant activity to reset timer
    local padding=$(printf '%*s' 600 '' | tr ' ' 'X')
    echo "{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"reset activity $padding\"}" >> "$transcript"

    # Wait another 4 seconds (total 8s, but only 4s since last activity)
    sleep 4

    # Sleeper should still be running (timer was reset)
    ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1

    # Clean up

test_sleeper_exits_after_activity_stops() {

SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=1
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=2
EOF

    # Set short timeout for testing (for current process)
    export SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=5
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
    export SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=1
    export SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=2

    local session_id="test-session-activity-stops"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id"

    # Create initial transcript
    local transcript="$TEST_DIR/transcript-stops.jsonl"
    echo '{"role":"user","content":"initial"}' > "$transcript"

    # Start sleeper
    topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript" "$TEST_DIR"
    sleep 0.5

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    [ -n "$pid" ] || return 1

    # Trigger 2-3 analyses with transcript changes
    for i in {1..3}; do
        sleep 2
        local padding=$(printf '%*s' 600 '' | tr ' ' 'X')
        echo "{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"activity $i $padding\"}" >> "$transcript"
    done

    # Verify sleeper is still running
    ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1 || return 1

    # Stop changing transcript and wait for timeout + buffer
    sleep 7

    # Sleeper should have exited due to inactivity
    ! ps -p "$pid" > /dev/null 2>&1

    # PID file should be cleaned up
    [ ! -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/$session_id/sleeper.pid" ]
}

# ============================================================================
# Main test execution
# ============================================================================

main() {
    echo "Running topic extraction feature tests..."
    echo
    echo "NOTE: These are TDD tests - functions may not be implemented yet."
    echo "Tests will be skipped if functions don't exist."
    echo

    setup

    # Clarity extraction tests
    run_test test_get_clarity_from_valid_file
    run_test test_get_clarity_missing_file
    run_test test_get_clarity_invalid_json

    # Analysis tests
    run_test test_analyze_creates_topic_file
    run_test test_analyze_respects_mode_topic_only
    # TODO: Fix this test - has JSON parsing issues
    # run_test test_analyze_respects_mode_full_analytics
    run_test test_analyze_handles_llm_failure

    # Cadence tests
    run_test test_check_cadence_high_clarity
    run_test test_check_cadence_low_clarity
    run_test test_check_cadence_no_topic_file

    # Sleeper tests
    run_test test_sleeper_start_creates_pid_file
    run_test test_sleeper_start_doesnt_duplicate
    run_test test_sleeper_disabled

    # Sleeper inactivity timeout tests
    run_test test_sleeper_exits_after_inactivity_timeout
    run_test test_sleeper_stays_alive_with_ongoing_activity
    run_test test_sleeper_activity_resets_inactivity_timer
    run_test test_sleeper_exits_after_activity_stops

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        echo
        echo "This is expected for TDD - implement the feature to pass tests."
        exit 0  # Don't fail on unimplemented features
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"
